message:
refactor: revert fts_indexed_count rename + clippy-clean dispatch cfg branches

Pair of small follow-ups to 1ad5c8cc (the three-fix commit) tracked
together because each is mechanical.

## 1. Revert PaneIndexingStats.fts_indexed_count → back to fts_row_count

The rename to `fts_indexed_count` I made in 1ad5c8cc ran into
downstream callers that still referenced `fts_row_count` and hadn't
been discovered by the grep audit (lint-generated callers, macro
expansion in another crate). Rather than chase every site, revert
the field name back to its original `fts_row_count` — the semantic
gap between "FTS rows" and "FTS-indexed segments" was the
motivating concern, but the field name is bikeshed territory when
the alternative is fighting the workspace compile.

The real fix from 1ad5c8cc — the `fts_match_count` test helper
rewrite that uses `WHERE output_segments_fts MATCH ?1` instead of
the broken `SELECT COUNT(*)` over an external-content table — is
preserved. That was the actual correctness win; the field rename
was a tangent.

Three call sites updated: struct field, build_indexing_health_report
fold, two storage_handle_tests fixture constructions. Zero behavior
change.

## 2. dispatch.rs: clippy-clean the cfg-gated readiness_default and
## wait_for_dispatch_{readable,writable}

Two separate clippy findings in the same file:

(a) `DispatchIoBackend::readiness_default` had nested
    `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` and target_os-union blocks each
    containing an explicit `return Self::Variant;`, followed by an
    unreachable bare `Self::Poll` tail. clippy::needless_return flags
    the explicit `return` inside branches that are the last expression
    of a cfg block. Restructure with a third cfg(not(any(...)))
    branch containing the Poll tail directly, turning each branch
    into an implicit-expression cfg block:

        #[cfg(target_os = "linux")] { Self::Epoll }
        #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", ...))] { Self::Kqueue }
        #[cfg(not(any(...)))] { Self::Poll }

    The three-way cfg partition covers every platform exhaustively
    and clippy stops complaining.

(b) `wait_for_dispatch_readable` and `wait_for_dispatch_writable`
    had an `io_uring_runtime` parameter that was only used under
    `cfg(all(feature = "io-uring", target_os = "linux"))`. Without
    those features, the parameter was unused and clippy::unused_variables
    warned. Rename to `_io_uring_runtime` so the convention-based
    suppression kicks in and the cfg-enabled code still binds the
    usable name via shadowing inside the cfg block.

No behavior change for either finding. All three blocks still
generate the same target-specific default on each supported platform.

## Beads

File ft-0pcn5 open/P2/feature: "escape parser short DCS streaming
recovery coverage". Pure metadata; no source touched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-0c8wp","title":"Correct README.md numerical claims: workspace crate count, vendored crate count, and module count","description":"# Goal
Fix the overstated numbers in README.md so the documentation matches reality. Documentation credibility matters — inflated numbers undermine trust in claims that ARE accurate.

# Background
The 2026-04-06 reality check found:
- README claims "120 workspace crates" → Actual: 63 workspace members in root Cargo.toml
- README claims "105 crates" from ex-WezTerm → Actual: 45 active vendored crates in frankenterm/ directory (95+ if counting legacy_wezterm/ checkpoint that isn't part of the workspace)
- README claims "482 modules" → Actual: 483 .rs files (off by 1, essentially correct)
- README claims "775k lines" → Actual: 793,698 lines (grew since README was written)
- README claims "45,000+ tests" → Actual: 46,633 (accurate, slightly understated)

# Changes Required
1. Update "120 workspace crates" to actual count. Options:
   a. "63 workspace crates" (strict workspace members)
   b. "63 workspace crates (plus 95+ legacy reference crates)" (honest with context)
2. Update vendored crate reference to "45 active vendored crates"
3. Update module count to 483 (or "480+" for durability)
4. Update line count to ~794k (or "790k+" for durability)
5. Keep test count as "45,000+" (still accurate)
6. Add a "last verified" date footnote for numerical claims

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Every numerical claim in README.md matches a verifiable count.
2. No claim is inflated. Rounding is always down or uses "N+" notation.
3. Badge at top of README matches actual numbers.
4. AGENTS.md workspace structure section is consistent with README.

# Implementation Notes
- The exact count will drift as development continues, so use "N+" notation for fast-moving numbers.
- The badge "Lines of Code" currently says "775k lines" — update to match.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:20.592960Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:46:03.711999Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:46:03.711832Z","close_reason":"Updated README summary counts to verified current values","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accuracy","docs","readme"]}
 {"id":"ft-0clof","title":"Pin policy rate limiter cross-scope quota accounting","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:47.364387Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:25.576056Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:25.573006Z","close_reason":"Landed policy rate-limiter cross-scope conformance tests in policy.rs. Commit also contains concurrent policy.rs hunks already present at staging time (ft-yjt9e/ft-3l5bu comments); not unwound here to avoid reverting another pane's work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-0e179","title":"[mor:distributed-systems] backpressure drops lose per-pane attribution","description":"# Observation

`BackpressureMetrics::segments_dropped` (crates/frankenterm-core/src/backpressure.rs:224)
is a single `AtomicU64`. When red/black-tier capture shedding fires and
segments are dropped, operators see **how many** were lost but not
**from which pane**.

In an incident ("rate limit slipped past detection at 02:14"), the
on-call needs to determine whether the dropped segment interval
included the pane they're debugging. Today that's un-answerable — the
drops counter is scalar.

# Question

Should we shift `segments_dropped` to a per-pane `AtomicU64` (keyed by
pane_id) so a 3am operator can pivot from "27 segments dropped in the
last minute" to "23 of those were pane 4167 (claude-code), 4 were pane
4203 (codex)"? Memory cost is bounded (≤ 8 bytes × N_panes).

Adjacent thought: the gap marker emitted to storage (`gaps_emitted`)
presumably already carries pane identity; could we expose a thin
aggregate view over gap markers instead of teaching backpressure to
track per-pane, keeping the drop-path allocation-free?

# CAP stance

Current design: chose availability (keep writing, drop segments when
queue full) over consistency (refuse writes to preserve ordering). The
tradeoff is sound for a terminal observation platform — we want the
next byte more than the missed one. But per-pane attribution turns
lost observations into *known* lost observations, which is the
difference between a bug report and a silent false-negative.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:33.894245Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:56:59.638838Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:56:59.638045Z","close_reason":"Fixed in bd8a715e: BackpressureMetrics gains per-pane HashMap (dropped_by_pane: RwLock<HashMap<u64, AtomicU64>>) + 4-method read surface (total, per-pane, snapshot, distinct-pane count). RuntimeMetrics.backpressure: Arc<BackpressureMetrics> threads the metric to native-event drop sites in runtime.rs::emit_native_output_delta and handle_native_event. 6 regression tests including concurrent-drop stress. Changes bundled into the concurrent ft-fbee5 commit during a race; code is in main. native_events.rs EventDispatchOutcome::Backpressure drop site left unwired as a follow-up (no RuntimeMetrics reach from that dispatcher).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distributed-systems","mor","observability"]}
+{"id":"ft-0pcn5","title":"escape parser short DCS streaming recovery coverage","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:24:14.038314Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:24:18.084906Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-0vt3k","title":"[SEV] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology rollback check only compares pane id set, not split geometry","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology Phase 5 (rewritten in 30172171 to fix ft-olq2e) now derives post_rollback_pane_ids from server.registry().snapshot() and asserts the sorted id list matches baseline_pane_ids. This is stronger than the previous vacuous baseline-JSON roundtrip, but it still only validates the *set* of surviving pane ids, not the restored split tree geometry: split ratios, split directions (horizontal vs vertical), parent/child edges, pane widths/heights, or the z-order of panes within the topology. A broken rollback that restores the correct 2 pane ids but swaps their split direction, scales their sizes, or re-parents them under a different split node would still pass this assertion. Metamorphic invariant: TopologySnapshot::from_registry(post_rollback) == TopologySnapshot::from_registry(pre_expansion) — not currently asserted. Fix by capturing a full TopologySnapshot before Phase 3 expansion, then after rollback compare structural fields (split tree, ratios, per-pane dimensions) in addition to pane id set. The existing 'restored_topo = from_json(&initial_topo_json)' on line 422 still only proves serde roundtrip; it does not compare to the live post-rollback state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:42.691288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912477Z","close_reason":"Resolved in 4784c64f + d29498d6. d29498d6 added TopologyFingerprint covering HSplit/VSplit direction, per-split child ratios (encode_ratio to u16), per-leaf pane_id/rows/cols, tab_id, active_pane_id, window_id, and active_tab_index; Phase 5 now asserts post_rollback_fingerprint == initial_fingerprint AND != expanded_fingerprint, so a rollback that restores the correct pane id set but swaps split direction, rescales panes, or re-parents nodes fails. 4784c64f tightened the check further with a direct TopologySnapshot equality (post_rollback_topo == &initial_topo) as the primary assertion and baseline-JSON == initial_topo as the secondary. Ran the test at HEAD: binary compiles clean; runtime verification on local host blocked by macOS dyld bottleneck under concurrent-agent compile load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-12e8l","title":"[SEV] mux-server Alert-after-PaneRemoved re-inserts HashMap entry, permanent leak","description":"crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs:256 processes MuxNotification::Alert by calling handler.per_pane(pane_id), which routes to SessionHandler::per_pane at sessionhandler.rs:282. That function uses HashMap::entry(..).or_insert_with(..) — a get-or-insert. When a MuxNotification::Alert arrives for a pane whose entry was already cleared by Item::Notif(MuxNotification::PaneRemoved) at dispatch.rs:247, the or_insert_with re-creates a fresh PerPane entry. The pane is already gone from mux, so no subsequent PaneRemoved will fire for that pane_id — the entry is a permanent leak.

Concrete interleaving:
  1. Mux fires PaneRemoved(1) followed by (or interleaved with) a delayed Alert{pane_id:1, PaletteChanged} that was enqueued by a config-reload path or a termwiz late-palette flush.
  2. Dispatch loop processes PaneRemoved -> handler.remove_per_pane(1) clears the map entry (line 247).
  3. Dispatch loop processes Alert -> handler.per_pane(1) -> or_insert_with -> re-creates an entry (line 256).
  4. Line 262 schedule_pane_push(1) likewise calls per_pane() internally, reinforcing the stale entry.
  5. No further PaneRemoved for pane 1 is ever emitted (pane already killed). Entry leaks for the lifetime of the ClientSession.

Severity: unbounded growth proportional to (alerts-after-removal × lifetime). Long-lived mux-server clients (editor sessions, persistent SSH mux) with high pane churn accumulate stale entries. Each entry holds a PerPane with notifications Vec, RenderableDimensions, and SurfaceCopy — not large individually but multiplied by churn + uptime, it matters.

Fix: expose a non-inserting accessor SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present(pane_id) -> Option<Arc<Mutex<PerPane>>> and use it for the Alert arm. If the pane isn't tracked, drop the Alert (the client can't render a dead pane anyway). Keep the or_insert_with pathway for client-initiated PDU arms (SendKeyDown, SendPaste, etc.) where insertion on first reference is correct. Also skip the schedule_pane_push for a pane that was just removed (line 262) — same reasoning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:46:06.681760Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:49:18.407372Z","close_reason":"Completed: added SessionHandler::per_pane_if_present non-inserting accessor and routed dispatch.rs Alert arm through it. Alert-after-PaneRemoved now drops the stale alert silently instead of re-creating a permanent map entry. Code changes landed in commit 3b45408d (another agent's codec-fuzz commit bundled my dispatch.rs + sessionhandler.rs edits into their push — my 37 lines are present in HEAD). cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --offline passed clean in 13.28s during my local verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.28","title":"[gui][test] GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite","description":"# GPU rendering tests and visual regression suite

## What
Automated tests for the wgpu rendering pipeline: correctness, visual regression detection, and performance validation.

## Why
GPU rendering bugs are invisible to unit tests — they produce wrong pixels, not wrong values. We need screenshot-based comparison to catch rendering regressions in text layout, cursor position, selection highlighting, split pane borders, and floating pane overlays.

## Tests

### Unit Tests (no GPU required)
- `test_cell_vertex_generation` — cell (row, col) produces correct vertex positions
- `test_atlas_rect_lookup` — glyph lookup returns correct UV coordinates
- `test_atlas_eviction_lru` — LRU evicts least-recently-used glyphs
- `test_dirty_rect_merge` — adjacent dirty cells merge into single rect
- `test_dirty_rect_clear_on_present` — dirty rects cleared after frame

### Visual Regression Tests (headless GPU or software renderer)
- `visual_basic_text` — "Hello, World!" renders correctly
- `visual_unicode_cjk` — CJK characters render at double width
- `visual_emoji` — emoji renders correctly (with fallback glyph)
- `visual_box_drawing` — box-drawing characters connect seamlessly
- `visual_cursor_shapes` — block, bar, underline cursor shapes
- `visual_selection_highlight` — selected text has correct background color
- `visual_split_pane_borders` — split pane borders render as clean lines
- `visual_floating_pane` — floating pane renders with shadow/border above tiled panes
- `visual_50_panes` — 50 split panes all render legible text

### Performance Tests
- `perf_frame_time_single_pane` — full-screen pane, measure frame time (target: < 4ms)
- `perf_frame_time_50_panes` — 50 panes, measure frame time (target: < 16ms)
- `perf_atlas_warmup` — time to cache 200 unique glyphs (target: < 100ms)
- `perf_scroll_fps` — continuous scroll, measure sustained fps (target: 60fps)

### E2E Script: `scripts/e2e_gpu_rendering.sh`
```
1. Launch frankenterm-gui in headless mode (--headless)
2. Open shell, run: echo "RENDER_TEST_$(date +%s)"
3. Capture screenshot to /tmp/ft_visual_test.png
4. Compare against golden screenshot (perceptual hash, tolerance for font differences)
5. Open 10 split panes, capture screenshot
6. Verify all 10 panes contain visible text (OCR or pixel sampling)
7. Measure frame time over 100 frames, report p50/p95/p99
8. Report: pass/fail with visual diff images on failure
```

## Golden Screenshot Management
- Golden screenshots stored in `tests/golden/gpu/`
- Updated via `BLESS=1 cargo test` (like insta crate)
- Platform-specific goldens: `tests/golden/gpu/macos/`, `tests/golden/gpu/linux/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visual regression suite runs in CI headlessly
- Frame time p99 < 16ms for single pane
- Font atlas handles full Unicode BMP without crash
- Visual diff images attached to failing CI runs for diagnosis","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-02T18:24:37.252309Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T05:38:44.195459Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","gui","rendering","testing"],"comments":[{"id":2635,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote taking a bounded support slice under the blocked GPU regression-suite bead: expand pure renderer helper/unit coverage in existing test modules (`termwindow/webgpu.rs` and `termwindow/render/mod.rs`) without pretending the full headless screenshot suite is ready before `ft-1memj.10` stabilizes. Goal: tighten edge-case coverage around surface-format/extent selection and render color/cursor helper invariants so the eventual visual-regression harness has a firmer correctness floor.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:45:45Z"},{"id":2637,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"RoseCoyote","text":"RoseCoyote progress: added focused renderer helper edge-case tests in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs and crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs. New coverage pins alpha-mode empty-capability fallback, per-axis surface extent clamping, explicit palette-vs-style foreground precedence, bright ANSI palette handling under bold intensity, and reverse-video cursor exact-threshold semantics. Validation: `cargo fmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed. `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-gui termwindow::webgpu::tests` failed on worker `vmi1293453` with `RCH-E307` because `x11.pc` is missing from the worker image; companion `termwindow::render::tests` run fell open local after `RCH-E324` and then stalled in an uninterruptible wait, so I terminated that process rather than leave a useless local compile hanging. Bead remains in_progress because the full GPU regression suite still depends on ft-1memj.10 and proper worker images.","created_at":"2026-03-31T02:52:53Z"},{"id":3521,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook taking a bounded ft-1memj.28 slice while Agent Mail is timing out under DB contention. Scope: audit the existing GPU renderer helper test surface in crates/frankenterm-gui and land a non-overlapping correctness/regression slice that improves renderer invariants without pretending the full headless visual suite is ready.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:27:47Z"},{"id":3526,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large and independent-axis clamping. Remote targeted verification reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing x11-xcb.pc for crate x11. Local rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check are clean.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:37:10Z"},{"id":3527,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.28","author":"CopperBrook","text":"CopperBrook support-slice update: added a resize-path surface-extent helper in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs so live-resize keeps zero-size minimize semantics but clamps oversized usize dimensions instead of truncating them into wrapped u32s. Added focused tests for zero-preserving resize extents plus large/independent-axis clamping. Verification: remote targeted lane  reached real compilation on worker vmi1149989 but failed before test execution because the worker image is missing  for crate  (-I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libx11/1.8.13/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/xorgproto/2025.1/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxcb/1.17.0/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxau/1.0.12/include -I/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxdmcp/1.1.5/include -L/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libx11/1.8.13/lib -lX11-xcb -lX11 -L/opt/homebrew/Cellar/libxcb/1.17.0/lib -lxcb failed). Local non-cargo hygiene is clean: rustfmt --check on the touched file and git diff --check pass.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:38:28Z"}]}
@@ -82,6 +83,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-atcr0","title":"backpressure::classify silently returns Green when config thresholds are NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/backpressure.rs` at `BackpressureManager::classify` (lines 416-438):

```rust
if capture_saturated || write_saturated {
    BackpressureTier::Black
} else if cr >= self.config.red_capture || wr >= self.config.red_write {
    BackpressureTier::Red
} else if cr >= self.config.yellow_capture || wr >= self.config.yellow_write {
    BackpressureTier::Yellow
} else {
    BackpressureTier::Green
}
```

Any `>=` comparison with NaN is false. If the operator ships a BackpressureConfig where one or more thresholds is NaN (TOML supports `yellow_capture = nan`, `red_capture = nan`, etc.), every branch falls through to Green. The backpressure monitor reports 'healthy' regardless of actual queue depth — disabling every upgrade action (pane-pause, detection skipping, capacity shedding).

# Parallel to ft-761tz

This is the same shape as c5a02edb (ft-761tz, disk_pressure):
- disk_pressure `classify_tier` silently returned Green on NaN usage_fraction — fixed by fail-closed Black return at the top of classify_tier.
- EwmaEstimator / PidController silently propagated NaN through clamp — fixed by NaN guard at update.

backpressure.rs has not received the same hardening. Config thresholds are `f64` and deserialized via serde; TOML's `nan` literal slips through unchanged. No `validate()` method exists on BackpressureConfig.

# Fix

1. Add a `BackpressureConfig::validate()` method that rejects non-finite thresholds, negative values, and yellow ≥ red misordering. Emit a descriptive error.
2. Defense-in-depth inside `classify`: if ANY relevant config threshold or input ratio is non-finite, return `BackpressureTier::Black` to fail closed (same disposition as ft-761tz).
3. Regression tests:
   - config_validate_rejects_nan_yellow_capture
   - config_validate_rejects_red_below_yellow
   - classify_returns_black_on_nan_config_threshold (defense-in-depth path)

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires operator misconfiguration to trigger, but the failure mode is silent (no panic, no error) and the blast radius is 'backpressure is effectively disabled' which can let the watcher runaway into OOM under load. The ft-761tz fix landed as HIGH; applying the same shape here keeps the fleet's failure model consistent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:03:47.755984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:08:40.773267Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): NaN-proof backpressure)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-auwbi","title":"rch verifier fails: openssl-sys build/main.rs:316 missing /opt/homebrew clang on Linux worker","description":"Verifier command:
`rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p4-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib pre_start_cancelled_cx_fails_execution_and_releases_lock`

Observed failure:
- `openssl-sys v0.9.112` build script panic at `build/main.rs:316:13`
- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`
- cargo surfaced: `error: failed to run custom build command for openssl-sys v0.9.112`

This is a worker-env mismatch: the requested macOS Homebrew clang path does not exist on the Linux rch worker, so verification fails before reaching frankenterm-core test code.","notes":"2026-04-22 tick-5 backpressure verifier:
- command: `rch exec -- env CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p8-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib classify_small_write_capacity_requires_high_fill_for_black`
- worker: `vmi1149989`
- cargo failed before frankenterm-core with `openssl-sys v0.9.112` build script panic at `/home/ubuntu/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/openssl-sys-0.9.112/build/main.rs:316:13`
- exact tool error: `failed to find tool "/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang": No such file or directory (os error 2)`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:12:32.293332Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.056230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-azjol","title":"[sec] ipc.rs: non-constant-time token compare (HIGH)","description":"CVSS v3.1 AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N ~5.7. Fixed in 5810a3a4. IpcAuth::authorize() used String::eq which short-circuits on first byte mismatch, plus .find() which short-circuits on first candidate match. Both are exploitable via timing side-channel over Unix socket: co-tenant process can recover the stored IPC token byte-by-byte AND distinguish token position in the vec. Fix: constant-time byte XOR accumulator in ipc_constant_time_eq + explicit loop in authorize() that visits every candidate. 7 regression tests. Found during /security-audit-for-saas adapted for CLI control-plane (pane6 phase C).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:27.060545Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327699Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5810a3a4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-b4kpu","title":"Fix no-default-features lib blockers in search_explain and storage FTS helpers","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:16.262625Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:16.262625Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b4l62","title":"[MEDIUM] telemetry Histogram NaN-poisons total_sum/mean and emits nonsense quantiles on NaN q","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure paths

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs`, `struct Histogram`.

### 1. `Histogram::record(NaN)` permanently poisons the running mean

```rust
pub fn record(&mut self, value: f64) {
    self.total_count += 1;
    self.total_sum += value;    // NaN poisons total_sum permanently
    if value < self.min { self.min = value; }
    if value > self.max { self.max = value; }
    ...
}
```

Once any recorded value is NaN, `total_sum` is NaN forever (clean samples do nothing to restore it). Then `mean()` returns `Some(NaN)`:

```rust
pub fn mean(&self) -> Option<f64> {
    if self.total_count == 0 { return None; }
    Some(self.total_sum / self.total_count as f64)
}
```

A silently-wrong mean propagates into every `HistogramSummary`, JSON export, and dashboard forever.

### 2. `Histogram::quantile(NaN)` returns an arbitrary sample

```rust
let idx = ((sorted.len() as f64 - 1.0) * q.clamp(0.0, 1.0)) as usize;
```

`f64::clamp(NaN, 0.0, 1.0)` returns NaN (documented gotcha). `(NaN) as usize` is 0 in Rust. So `quantile(NaN)` returns `Some(sorted[0])` — an arbitrary 'smallest' sample rather than `None`. Combined with a NaN sample in the retained window, sort order is non-deterministic, and the output is effectively undefined.

### 3. Classify: silent wrong-answer

Not a panic, not data loss — operators looking at a dashboard see a mean that says NaN (or a quantile that says zero) and assume the subsystem is healthy.

## Reachability

`Histogram::record` is called from `MetricRegistry::record_histogram` which takes an f64. Callers that pass `dt.as_secs_f64()` are safe in practice, but any caller doing arithmetic (rate = delta_samples / delta_time with delta_time = 0.0) can produce NaN. Same class as ft-761tz (disk_pressure clamp-doesn't-sanitise-NaN) and ft-atcr0 (backpressure config validate).

## Fix

1. `Histogram::record`: if `value.is_nan()`, return early (no counter bump, no sum update, no sample push). This matches ft-761tz's fail-closed precedent.
2. `Histogram::quantile`: if `q.is_nan()`, return `None` explicitly — don't let the `clamp` + `as usize` cast silently produce index 0.
3. Regression tests covering both defenses.

## Scope

Single file (`telemetry.rs`). No public API change (NaN input is still accepted, just no longer poisonous).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:39:42.897340Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:08.381499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:08.380804Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}

 {"id":"ft-phbu5","title":"[HIGH] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology still cannot verify pane-tree rollback","description":"Commit 30172171 fixes the vacuous baseline roundtrip, but the test still reduces "topology" to a pane-id set derived from LifecycleRegistry records rather than the restored topology payload.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs:392-423` now compares only `server.registry().snapshot()` pane IDs/count against the baseline and then re-parses `initial_topo_json` as a secondary serde check.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs:38-89` defines topology as windows, tabs, `active_tab_index`, and recursive `pane_tree`, not just pane membership.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/durable_state.rs:159-167,288-314` shows HeadlessMuxServer checkpoints/rollback persist and rebuild only `LifecycleRegistry::snapshot()` entities; there is no topology JSON in the checkpoint path for the test to compare after rollback.

Why this matters:
- A rollback that preserves the same pane IDs but restores the wrong tab grouping, active tab, or split tree would still satisfy the current assertions.
- The test name and comments still claim topology restoration coverage, so this is false confidence on the checkpoint/rollback surface.

Expected fix:
- Either persist/restore topology payloads in the checkpoint path and compare a post-rollback `TopologySnapshot` against the baseline,
- or explicitly narrow/rename this test to registry-membership rollback and add a separate topology persistence/restore integration test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:58:05.819749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:23:00.021289Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:23:00.020891Z","close_reason":"Strengthened full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology with structural pane-tree rollback invariant","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-phz7x","title":"codec: buffered_frame_len accepts oversized tagged_len, allowing memory-exhaustion before MAX_PDU_SIZE fires","description":"frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:240 buffered_frame_len validates only that the leb128-decoded payload_len fits in usize. It does NOT check against MAX_PDU_SIZE (256 MiB at lib.rs:106). An attacker who sends a frame whose outer tagged_len advertises e.g. 10 GiB forces try_read_and_decode at lib.rs:763-792 into a read loop that keeps extending the caller's buffer 4 KiB at a time until buffer.len() >= total_len — because stream_decode's first step is if buffered_frame_len(buffer)?.is_none() { return Ok(None); }, which short-circuits to 'need more bytes' and never rejects oversize. decode_raw at lib.rs:395-403 DOES reject via MAX_PDU_SIZE, but by then the process has already OOM'd or swapped. Fix: in buffered_frame_len, after decoding payload_len, early-bail if payload_len > MAX_PDU_SIZE so stream_decode returns Err immediately on an oversize header and callers stop accumulating.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:57.249807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:57.249807Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-qf3vk","title":"[HIGH] ft-ybtyg incomplete: session_checkpoints.state_hash is written but never verified on load","description":"Follow-up from ft-ybtyg (closed in 598a7c13).

## Gap

The ft-ybtyg fix replaced the placeholder `'restore'` literal with a real SipHash via `compute_restore_state_hash` and documented the intent: 'a later integrity check can recompute and compare'. But grep across session_restore.rs + snapshot_engine.rs + storage.rs finds ZERO callers that read the stored state_hash, recompute from the current inputs, and assert equality. The hash is written on INSERT, never verified on SELECT/load.

Result: the on-disk state_hash column now contains useful-looking SipHash values but is still effectively unverified — an attacker who tampers with the session_checkpoints row (flipping hash + any other field) is not detected, because no read path compares the stored hash against the recomputed hash.

## Reproducer
```
grep -rn 'compute_restore_state_hash\|compute_state_hash' crates/frankenterm-core/src/ | grep -iE 'verify|check|compare|assert_eq'
```
Expected matches: any call site that does `recomputed == stored`.
Actual: (empty).

## Fix direction
1. In the session-load path (session_restore.rs load fn + snapshot_engine.rs open/restore), after reading a session_checkpoints row, call compute_restore_state_hash on the current inputs and compare with the stored state_hash column.
2. On mismatch, return a typed Error (new StateHashMismatch variant) and refuse to restore from a tampered checkpoint.
3. Add regression test with a deliberately-tampered row that must produce StateHashMismatch and NOT a silent restore.

## Severity
MEDIUM → HIGH. The fix closed the docs-drift half but left the verification half open. A partial integrity check is worse than no check because it signals a stronger trust story than actually exists.

## Priority
P1 — this closes a real hole that ft-ybtyg was supposed to close.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:03:26.056799Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:28:42.029163Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:28:42.028931Z","close_reason":"fixed in ca3d3fe2 — state_hash verification wired into load_checkpoint_by_id for startup rows; StateHashMismatch error + 5 regression tests (tampered hash, tampered pane_id_map, legacy 'restore' literal, null metadata, non-startup skip). Periodic/capture checkpoints remain a documented follow-up since their hash is over PaneInfo not reconstructable from session_checkpoints alone.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-rrrn5","title":"[SEV] CommandBuilder env_map_serde silently collides duplicate keys after to_string_lossy","description":"frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs env_map_serde (added in 803356bf) maps BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry> -> JSON via OsString::to_string_lossy for keys. Two distinct OsString keys whose non-UTF-8 bytes differ but collapse to the same String under lossy conversion will (1) produce a JSON map containing duplicate keys during serialize (most JSON parsers keep last, silently dropping one entry), and (2) on deserialize the BTreeMap::insert loop overwrites on collision, again silently dropping entries without returning an error. The adapter offers no detection or rejection of this case — a corrupted env set roundtrips to a smaller env set with no diagnostic. Metamorphic property: |deserialize(serialize(m))| == |m| — currently violated for any m with keys that collide under to_string_lossy. Conformance concern: the commit message asserts 'POSIX env names are ASCII-only' but setenv(3) on Linux accepts non-ASCII bytes (LC_*=ja_JP.UTF-8 etc.), and libc-level execve passes raw bytes. Fix by either (a) erroring on lossy conversion in both directions, or (b) serializing as an array of [key_bytes, value] pairs via serde_bytes / base64 so the roundtrip is lossless. At minimum add a proptest that asserts roundtrip preserves map size when two synthesized OsString keys share a lossy-equivalent representation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:30.464855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565957Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:35:11.565787Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 7a452b96: replaced map adapter with sequence-of-pairs adapter so every distinct OsString key survives serde roundtrip losslessly (via OsString's native byte/UTF-16-preserving enum encoding in value position). Added 2 regression proptests: one generates arbitrary byte keys (many non-UTF-8) and asserts all distinct keys roundtrip with paired values intact; the other injects two different invalid-UTF-8 sequences that both lossy-render to U+FFFD and asserts both survive. Test-binary run is blocked by pane6-target lock contention; fix is structurally complete and compiles clean at my previous build pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-s2u51","title":"asupersync dep graph split: crates.io 0.2.9 + git HEAD both present, breaks fastapi/fastmcp bridges","description":"cargo check reports 'there are multiple different versions of crate asupersync in the dependency graph' for web_framework.rs:180 (ResponseBody::stream needs asupersync 0.2.9's Stream, code has git Stream) and mcp_tools.rs:4759 (McpContext::new needs fastmcp_rust's asupersync Cx, code passes git asupersync's Cx). +{"id":"ft-s6l5b","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin match_rule_glob invariants under proptest (empty patterns, star-wildcards, length preservation)","description":"# Gap

events.rs:1121 `pub fn match_rule_glob(pattern, value) -> bool` —
a hand-rolled glob matcher with `*` (any sequence) and `?` (any
single char) support. 19 inline example tests at events.rs:2827+ cover
specific cases. No proptest / property-based coverage exists.

# Risk

Hand-rolled glob matchers are classic defect surfaces — backtrack
state machines have edge cases that example tests routinely miss:

1. Empty-pattern vs empty-value semantics (false positives here
   would break exclude rules)
2. Pattern of N `?`s must match exactly N-char values (length-
   preservation invariant that's easy to regress with an off-by-one)
3. `"*"` alone must match every value (including empty) — this is
   the "allow-everything" escape hatch operators rely on
4. Exact patterns (no wildcards) must be length-preserving: only
   match same-length values with same bytes
5. Idempotence: `match_rule_glob(x, x) == true` for any exact pattern x

# Fix

New proptest suite in events.rs test module with 5 invariant properties,
each bearing the domain check the hand-rolled state machine is supposed
to guarantee. Runs under the existing module's test harness — no new
test file or feature-gate.

# Catches

- Backtrack-reset off-by-one at events.rs:1144 (p_star assignment
  wrong-side) would fail invariant #1/#3
- Wildcard-in-early-exit regression at :1122 would fail #4
- Star-at-end (`"a*"`) improperly requiring trailing chars would
  fail #3 on pattern="a??" value="abc"

Shipping as a net-positive guard, not a known-bug fix.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:36.694100Z","created_by":"pane3-glob-proptest","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:36.694100Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["events","proptest","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-s9oci","title":"mux/domain follow-ups: blocking spawn_command in async, silent write! errors","description":"Deferred during /multi-pass-bug-hunting audit shipped as dcaf60ca. Three pass-1/2 findings were clean in domain.rs, but two design-level issues remain as follow-up work:

1. Blocking spawn inside async fn. LocalDomain::spawn_pane (domain.rs:667) calls pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd) which on Unix is a synchronous fork+exec. If the child binary or an interposed fork hook hangs, the async task's executor thread is blocked indefinitely. Fix direction: route the spawn through tokio::task::spawn_blocking (or the asupersync equivalent) and await the join handle. Scope: also audit pair.master.take_writer() on the same path.

2. Silently swallowed write! errors. LocalDomain::spawn_pane on the child-spawn-failure branch (domain.rs:693) renders the error into the new pane via write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok(). If the write itself fails the user sees a blank pane with no indication of the upstream spawn failure. Fix direction: log the nested error via tracing::warn! with both the original spawn err and the write err so the failure is at least visible in logs.

Auditor: pane 6 (2026-04-20). Audit context: 91% context budget, committed primary fix + this bead for the residual scope before /clear.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:59:03.628753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409355Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409051Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5c8c417c. Item 1: spawn_command now runs via promise::spawn::spawn_into_new_thread so fork+exec no longer blocks the async executor thread. Item 2: silent write! errors were already fixed earlier in f6019f0f (replaced .ok() with and_then(flush)+tracing::warn! on both err paths).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async-safety","domain","follow-up","mux"]}
